title: The Present Subjunctive
description: Form the present subjunctive from the ils-stem, learn the key irregular verbs, and use it after triggers like il faut que, vouloir que, bien que and pour que.

cards.back: build the subjunctive from the ils-form: drop -ent, add -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent
cards.notes: Take the **ils** present form (*parlent*, *finissent*, *vendent*), drop **-ent**, and add the subjunctive endings.
Nous/vous look like the imparfait (*que nous parlions*, *que vous parliez*).

cards.back: (that) I do / make — subjunctive of faire
cards.notes: Irregular stem **fass-**: que je fasse, que tu fasses, qu'il fasse…
Irregular: aille, ailles, aille, **allions, alliez**, aillent.
être: sois, sois, soit, soyons, soyez, soient. avoir: aie, aies, ait, ayons, ayez, aient.
The classic trigger: *Il faut que je parte.* — I have to leave.
*vouloir que* + subjunctive when the subject changes. No infinitive as in English.
*Bien qu'il soit fatigué, il travaille.* — Although he is tired, he works.
*Je t'explique pour que tu comprennes.* — I explain so that you understand.

steps.title: Forming the present subjunctive
steps.body:
# The present subjunctive

The subjunctive is a **mood** for what is wished, required, doubted or emotional — not a plain statement of fact.

## The rule: start from the ils-form

1. Take the **ils** present form: *parler → parlent*, *finir → finissent*, *vendre → vendent*.
2. Drop **-ent**.
3. Add the endings:

| | parler | finir |
|---|---|---|
| que je | parle | finisse |
| que tu | parles | finisses |
| qu'il/elle | parle | finisse |
| que nous | parlions | finissions |
| que vous | parliez | finissiez |
| qu'ils | parlent | finissent |

> *nous/vous* look exactly like the **imparfait**: *que nous parlions*.

steps.title: Irregular verbs and triggers
steps.body:
# Irregular subjunctives

Learn these by heart:

| Verb | Subjunctive |
|---|---|
| faire | **fasse** |
| aller | **aille** (but allions, alliez) |
| être | **sois**, soit, soyons, soient |
| avoir | **aie**, ait, ayons, aient |
| pouvoir | **puisse** |
| savoir | **sache** |

## When to use it — the triggers

The subjunctive appears in a **que-clause** after expressions of necessity, will, emotion or concession:

- **Il faut que** je parte. — I have to leave.
- **Je veux que** tu viennes. — I want you to come.
- **Bien qu'**il soit tard, … — Although it is late, …
- **Pour que** tu comprennes, … — So that you understand, …

Key: with **a change of subject**, French uses *que* + subjunctive where English often uses an infinitive (*I want you **to come*** → *Je veux que tu **viennes***).

exercise.prompt: Match each infinitive with its (irregular) je-subjunctive form.
Fill in the subjunctive of partir (je): "It is necessary that I leave."
Translate: *I want you to come.* (change of subject → que + subjunctive)
Fill in the subjunctive of être (il): "Although he is tired, he works."
Arrange: *I explain so that you understand.* (pour que + subjunctive)
exercise.hint: pour que + tu + subjunctive of comprendre (comprennes).

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I have to do it.* (Il faut que… + faire, je)
accept: Il faut que je le fasse.
il faut que je le fasse
Il faut que je le fasse
distractors: Il faut que je le fais.
Il faut que je le faire.
exercise.hint: Il faut que + je + le + subjunctive of faire (fasse).
